sections.sourcing: No Tier 1/2/3 factory list update was published on nextplc.co.uk this week, but a live sourcing-cost story is worth tracking: under the US Section 122 tariff regime, Bangladesh's rate has fallen to match Turkey's at 10%, erasing Turkey's previous tariff advantage over Next's largest South Asian hub. That 10% Section 122 rate is currently due to expire around 24 July 2026, so a US policy change in the next fortnight could quickly reshuffle relative cost competitiveness between two of Next's core sourcing countries.
sections.strategy: Follow-up commentary (Forbes, 7 July) framed Next's reported early-stage interest in Harvey Nichols as evidence of a broader shift: Next increasingly competes on logistics, tech and its Total Platform infrastructure rather than just mainline fashion, and a luxury department-store acquisition would let it capture spend across the full price spectrum, from accessible fashion to designer goods. Talks remain described as early stage; Harvey Nichols declined to comment and Next has not confirmed a bid.
sections.gossip: No new leak beyond last week's Sky News scoop on Harvey Nichols has surfaced; trade press this week largely re-analyzed the same story rather than reporting fresh developments, with no confirmation that discussions have advanced to a formal offer.
soWhat: The Harvey Nichols story hasn't progressed to a formal bid this week, but continued analyst framing of it as a 'platform-led' luxury move reinforces that Next's growth thesis now leans on Total Platform infrastructure and brand roll-ups as much as organic mainline sales — worth watching for eventual cross-brand sourcing volume if it closes.

Bangladesh's Section 122 tariff rate now matches Turkey's at 10%, narrowing a cost gap that has shaped sourcing allocation between the two countries; with that rate scheduled to expire around 24 July 2026, suppliers in both hubs should watch for a US policy update in the coming weeks that could reopen the gap either way.

No new Tier 1 supplier list, sustainability certification news (BCI/GOTS/GRS), or Total Platform brand update was published this week — direct supply-chain disclosures remain quiet; the next scheduled catalyst is Next's H1 trading/results cycle.
